pairs.question: How many times has the title 'Earl of Dover' been created, and in which peerages?
pairs.answer: The title 'Earl of Dover' has been created twice: once in the Peerage of England and once in the Jacobite Peerage.

pairs.question: Who was the first Earl of Dover in the Peerage of England, and when was he created?
pairs.answer: The first Earl of Dover in the Peerage of England was Henry Carey, who was created Earl of Dover in 1628.

pairs.question: What notable event occurred to the second Earl of Dover in 1640, and why was it significant?
pairs.answer: In 1640, the second Earl of Dover (son of Henry Carey) was summoned to the House of Lords through a writ of acceleration in his father's barony of Hunsdon. This was significant because it allowed him to take his seat in Parliament earlier than would have been possible through normal succession.

pairs.question: Why did the Earldom of Dover become extinct in the Peerage of England?
pairs.answer: The Earldom of Dover became extinct in the Peerage of England upon the death of the second Earl in 1677 because he had no heirs to succeed him.

pairs.question: Who was the first Earl of Dover in the Jacobite Peerage, and by whom was the title granted?
pairs.answer: The first Earl of Dover in the Jacobite Peerage was Henry Jermyn, and the title was granted by the deposed King James II in July 1689.

pairs.question: What additional titles did Henry Jermyn receive alongside becoming Earl of Dover in the Jacobite Peerage?
pairs.answer: Alongside becoming Earl of Dover, Henry Jermyn was also created Baron Jermyn of Royston, Baron Ipswich, and Viscount Cheveley by King James II.

pairs.question: Did the Jacobite titles granted to Henry Jermyn have official recognition in England? Explain.
pairs.answer: No, the Jacobite titles granted to Henry Jermyn were not officially recognized by the English Government, though he was commonly referred to as the Earl of Dover.

pairs.question: What role did Henry Jermyn play in the Battle of the Boyne, and how did his allegiance change afterward?
pairs.answer: Henry Jermyn commanded a troop at the Battle of the Boyne. Afterward, he made his submission to King William III and spent the rest of his life living quietly.

pairs.question: How did the Jacobite Earldom of Dover become extinct?
pairs.answer: The Jacobite Earldom of Dover became extinct in 1708 upon the death of Henry Jermyn because he left no children by his wife, Judith Poley.
